LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PNUEMO ABEX, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. THE SUPPLEMENTAL INTERROGATORY RESPONSES ARE ADMITTED. THE OMISSION IN THE MOVING PAPERS WAS DUE TO CLERICAL ERROR AND PLAINTIFF WAS NOT PREJUDICED FOR THE MOVING PAPERS REPEATEDLY DISCLOSED DEFENDANTS RELIANCE ON THE RESPONSES. DEFENDANT HAS SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCTION BASED ON THE INTERROGATORY RESPONSES AND PLAINTIFFS' DEPOSITION TESTIMONY. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PRESENTED EVIDENCE GIVING RISE TO A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO KENTFIELD DECLARATION NUMBERS 7, 8 AND 9 ARE SUSTAINED. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION TO JACKSON'S DECLARATION IS OVERRULED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-28-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES

Appearances:
Natalie Holden of McKenna Long & Aldridge (267-4127) for defendant and moving party

No other appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: granted. Plaintiff's objections to decedent's interrogatory answers are overruled for plaintiff is suing as decedent's successor in interest. Defendant has shifted the burden of producing evidence by reliance on decedent's interrogatory responses and the deposition testimony of plaintiff and decedent. This record reflects the inability of plaintiff to prove that decedent was exposed to asbestos from a product manufactured or supplied by defendant. Defendant's objection to the Newsock declaration is sustained. Defendant's objections to Ay's declaration appear well taken for the declaration lacks facts that establish that Ay is an expert on automotive repair work and/or any special knowledge as to defendant's products. Even assuming Ay's declaration is admissible, plaintiff fails to present evidence that creates a triable issue of fact. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

Appearances:
Michelle Clowser of Brydon Hugo & Parker (808-0300) for defendant and moving party

No other appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: granted. The supplemental interrogatory responses are admitted. The omission in the moving papers was due to clerical error and plaintiff was not prejudiced for the moving papers repeatedly disclosed defendants reliance on the responses. Defendant has shifted the burden of production based on the interrogatory responses and plaintiffs' deposition testimony. Plaintiff has not presented evidence giving rise to a triable issue of fact. Plaintiff's objections to Kentfield declaration numbers 7, 8 and 9 are sustained. Defendant's objection to Jackson's declaration is overruled. Order signed in open Court.
